body: DECLARE i int; BEGIN END
DECLARE x varchar(10); BEGIN END
DECLARE x numeric(10,2); BEGIN END
DECLARE x foo.bar%TYPE; BEGIN END
DECLARE x int[]; BEGIN END
DECLARE x CONSTANT int := 1; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
x CONSTANT int := 1;
BEGIN
END

body: DECLARE x int := 1 + 2; BEGIN END
DECLARE x int = 5; BEGIN END
DECLARE x int DEFAULT 7; BEGIN END
DECLARE x int := (SELECT count(*) FROM t); BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
x int := (SELECT count(*) FROM t);
BEGIN
END

body: DECLARE x int NOT NULL := 0; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
x int NOT NULL := 0;
BEGIN
END

body: DECLARE a int; b text := 'hi'; BEGIN END
DECLARE i int; BEGIN NULL; END

comment: NOT NULL without default is an error
body: DECLARE x int NOT NULL; BEGIN END
DECLARE x ALIAS FOR $1; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
x ALIAS FOR $1;
BEGIN
END

body: DECLARE x ALIAS FOR a.b; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
x ALIAS FOR a.b;
BEGIN
END

comment: empty DECLARE section (DECLARE with no declarations)
body: DECLARE BEGIN NULL; END

comment: empty DECLARE with a block label
body: <<b>> DECLARE BEGIN NULL; END
DECLARE x text COLLATE "C"; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
x text COLLATE "C";
BEGIN
END

comment: COLLATE with NOT NULL and default
body: DECLARE x text COLLATE "en_US" NOT NULL := 'a'; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
x text COLLATE "en_US" NOT NULL := 'a';
BEGIN
END
